summary: Fowler reflects on the 2012 goto conference in Aarhus, highlighting several emerging technology trends. He praises Netflix's openness in sharing their Cassandra learnings and tools, contrasting it with the secrecy that often hampers industry progress. The post focuses heavily on JavaScript's growing dominance despite its flaws, discussing TypeScript as a promising solution. He also notes Microsoft's significant shift toward open-source collaboration, marking a change from their historically hostile stance. Finally, he emphasizes SVG's potential to transform web visualization by making vector graphics accessible through the DOM.
key_points: Netflix exemplifies valuable industry openness by sharing tools and lessons from their large-scale Cassandra usage
JavaScript's centrality is growing despite being deeply flawed, making TypeScript an important development
Microsoft has shifted significantly toward open-source collaboration, with teams like ASP.NET/Azure and TypeScript embracing openness
The 'new data panorama' offers opportunities for developers to be active participants rather than passive recipients of requirements
SVG represents a profound shift by creating DOM-accessible vector elements that enable new possibilities for data visualization and interactivity
money_quotes: Too often our profession's progress has been held back by secrecy that's born more out of vanity than any real competitive advantage.
I'm one of those who despairs that a language with such deep flaws plays such an important role in computation.
It also provides an opportunity to push back against the sad tendency to see developers as a passive recipient of requirements.
We must take javascript seriously as a first-class language and concentrate on how to limit the damage its flaws cause.
My sense is that Microsoft is very much a factional organization, with many factions still deeply inimical to the open-source world.
SVG is more than a standard to get vector graphics on the web, the more important impact of SVG is that it creates vector shapes as elements in the DOM.
key_insight: Industry progress depends on companies sharing their learnings openly, and developers must engage actively with emerging technologies rather than passively receiving requirements.
